steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question. The researchers found a specific difference between wild and captive tucos: daytime is the most active period for wild individuals but not for captive ones. The blank must complete the conclusion the researchers drew from this finding — the most logical inference about what the lab setting was doing.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'more suitable than wild settings for studying tucos' patterns of rest and activity': The data show the lab produces different behavior than the wild, which means lab results are less representative, not more suitable. ✗
  • Choice B — 'affecting the results for both tucos and dark-eyed juncos': The passage only gives evidence about tucos. The conclusion cannot extend to dark-eyed juncos without evidence — this oversteps the data. ✗
  • Choice C — 'more suitable for studying dark-eyed juncos than for studying tucos': No data comparing suitability across species is provided. This introduces an unsupported comparison. ✗
  • Choice D — 'interfering with tucos' patterns of rest and activity': Wild tucos are most active during the day; captive ones are not. The most logical explanation is that the lab setting is disrupting — interfering with — their natural activity patterns. This follows directly from the evidence. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
